message:
test(readme): cover reindex/truncate/insert across Series + DataFrame (fd90.238 / br-5ax53)

Extends readme_element_wise_operations_compiles_and_runs to cover 5
reindex/truncate/insert methods that previously had no integration
coverage:

- Series.reindex(new_labels) — verified 6→8 padding with NaN
- Series.truncate(Some(&before), Some(&after)) — interval [1, 4]
- DataFrame.reindex — same on row axis
- DataFrame.truncate — same interval semantics
- DataFrame.insert(loc, name, column) — README line 1146 positional
  insert; asserts the new column lands at position 0

Verified: 35 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"frankenpandas-57id","title":"fd90.70: fp-frankentui needs crate-level rustdoc summarizing TUI snapshot/E2E surface","description":"Final crate in the fd90.61-69 doc audit. Add crate-level //! rustdoc to fp-frankentui - the FrankenTUI terminal interface for interactive fp-frame / frankenpandas workflows. Public surface centers on snapshot value types (PacketSnapshot, PolicySnapshot, ConformanceDashboardSnapshot, DriftHistorySnapshot, DifferentialValidationSnapshot etc.) and the FrankentuiE2e scenario harness (E2eScenario / E2eScenarioReport / E2eReplayBundleEntry). Doc explains the role (TUI dashboard for monitoring conformance / drift / decision policy / packet replay), main snapshot families, and the E2E scenario test harness. Pure docs. Verify cargo doc cleanly under RUSTDOCFLAGS=-D warnings.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"docs","assignee":"cc-pandas-2","created_at":"2026-04-26T06:37:54.241551181Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T06:40:24.220322050Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T06:40:24.217845893Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","fd90","review"]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-597l","title":"fd90.56: schema validation error says invalid table name; should say invalid schema name","description":"Several SQL builders validate the schema arg by calling validate_sql_table_name(schema). The shared identifier rule is the same so validation works correctly, but the error message says 'invalid table name: schemaXyz' when actually the SCHEMA name was bad - misleading. Now that fd90.55 has the kind-parameterized validate_sql_ident, fix is one-line per call site: add validate_sql_schema_name(s) -> validate_sql_ident(s, schema) thin wrapper, swap the table-name call at the schema validation sites. Roughly 6 call sites in sql_select_all_query_in_schema, sql_select_columns_query_in_schema, sql_create_table_query_in_schema, sql_insert_rows_query_in_schema, sql_drop_table_query_in_schema, sql_multi_row_insert_query_in_schema. Update the existing sql_select_all_query_in_schema_validates_schema_name test to assert the new error wording (invalid schema name).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cc-pandas-2","created_at":"2026-04-26T05:06:57.107235071Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T05:10:35.255223984Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T05:10:35.252730443Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bug","fd90","review","sql"]}

+{"id":"frankenpandas-5ax53","title":"fd90.238: Cover reindex/truncate/insert across Series + DataFrame","description":"fd90.238: Cover Series.reindex / Series.truncate / DataFrame.insert / DataFrame.reindex / DataFrame.truncate.

Five reindex/insert/truncate methods are uncovered:
- Series.reindex(new_labels: Vec<IndexLabel>) (line 2059)
- Series.truncate(Option<&IndexLabel>, Option<&IndexLabel>) (line 6122)
- DataFrame.reindex (line 20660)
- DataFrame.truncate (line 25585)
- DataFrame.insert(loc: usize, name, column) (line 25695) — README line 1146

Scope:
- Extend an existing test with all 5 calls.

 {"id":"frankenpandas-5h6n","title":"fp-types Timestamp floor_to/ceil_to/round_to (9p0u Phase 2.5)","description":"Phase 2.5 follow-up to br-9p0u (Timestamp scaffolding shipped in commit 574b4b5).

**Scope.** Add Timedelta-unit rounding methods on Timestamp:
- `Timestamp::floor_to(unit_nanos: i64) -> Timestamp` — round down to nearest multiple.
- `Timestamp::ceil_to(unit_nanos: i64) -> Timestamp` — round up.
- `Timestamp::round_to(unit_nanos: i64) -> Timestamp` — banker's rounding (half-to-even, matches pandas .round()).

**Reference behavior (pandas 2.2):**
- `pd.Timestamp('2024-01-01 12:34:56').floor('H')` → `2024-01-01 12:00:00`.
- `.ceil('H')` → `2024-01-01 13:00:00`.
- `.round('H')` → `2024-01-01 13:00:00` (banker's rounds half toward even).
- NaT → NaT for all three.
- Unit must divide pandas's representable range; nonsense units pandas rejects with ValueError.

**FrankenPandas state.** Phase 2 added `Timestamp` struct + add/sub Timedelta. No rounding helpers. Pure i64 arithmetic on nanos axis — no TZ math required (rounding-then-conversion vs convert-then-rounding can diverge for DST-crossing units, but the unit_nanos parameter constrains us to strictly numeric rounding which matches pandas's tz-naive semantics).

**Fix shape (Phase 2.5 — pure additive in fp-types):**
1. Three `pub fn` on Timestamp: `floor_to`, `ceil_to`, `round_to`.
2. Floor: `nanos.div_euclid(unit) * unit` (handles negative correctly).
3. Ceil: floor result + (unit if remainder != 0).
4. Round: distance-to-nearest-multiple; ties go to even multiple.
5. Zero/negative unit_nanos → returns NaT (matches pandas ValueError surface as NaT propagation).
6. Tests: 8-10 cases including positive/negative nanos, exact-multiple no-op, zero-unit rejection, NaT propagation.

**Blast radius.** Purely additive in fp-types. Zero call-site touches.

**Severity.** LOW — small sweetener for users porting pandas datetime rounding workflows. Phase 3 chrono_tz integration adds the tz-aware variant.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cc-pandas","created_at":"2026-04-25T04:52:53.061806703Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T04:55:16.030063734Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T04:55:16.027569973Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dtype","parity-gap","review","timestamp"]}
